sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, pr. to draw out, to pull out; نصل and نضل, Ethiop. ነጸለ፡ to take away. Kindred are נָשַׁל, שָׁלַל.

Piel-

(1) to take away, to strip off something, from some one, 2Ch 20:25.

(2) followed by an acc. of pers. to despoil any one, Exo 3:22 12:36 .

(3) to snatch out of danger, to preserve, Eze 14:14 see Hiph.

Hiphil הִצִּיל-

(1) to pull a way; followed by בֵּין to pull apart. 2Sa 14:6, “the boys strove together in the field וְאֵין מַצִּיל בֵּינֵיהֶם and there was not one to pull them apart;” niemand riß fie aus einander.

(2) i.q. Piel No. 1; to take away, to deprive of, Gen 31:9 followed by מִן verse 16 Gen 31:16 Psa 119:43. 2Sa 20:6, וְהִצִּיל עֵינֵנוּ “and (lest) he take away our eye,” i.e. withdraw himself from our eyes.

(3) to snatch, to deliver any one from danger; with an acc. of pers. (once with a dat. Jon 4:6); followed by מִן Psa 18:49 34:5 , 18 Psa 34:18 Mic 5:5 מִיַּד out of the hand, the power of any one, Gen 32:12 37:21 , 22 Gen 37:22 Exo 3:8 18:10 מִכַּף id. Isa 38:6 הִצִּיל נַפְשֹׁו to save one’s own life, Eze 3:19, 21 Eze 3:21אֵין מַצִּיל there is no deliverer, none aiding, Psa 7:3 50:22 Isa 5:29.

Hophal הֻצַּל to be snatched or plucked out, Amo 4:11 Zec 3:2.

Niphal-

(1) pass. of Hiphil No. 3, to be plucked out, to be delivered, Isa 20:6 Jer 7:10 Amo 3:12.

(2) to save oneself, to escape, Pro 6:3, . Followed by אֶל to escape to any one, Deu 23:16.

Hithpael, to strip oneself of any thing, with an acc. Exo 33:6 comp. Heb. Gram. § 53, 3, letter d.

Derivative הַצָּלָה.
